info.name: Runloop Blueprint API
info.description:
Build and manage Blueprints — Dockerfile-based container image templates for fast Devbox startup with pre-installed dependencies, system packages, and tooling. Supports building, previewing, listing public blueprints, and metadata-based discovery.

Contact Support:
 Name: Runloop AI Support
 Email: support@runloop.ai

item.name: Create and build a Blueprint from a RepositoryConnection Inspection.

request.description: Starts build of custom defined container Blueprint using a RepositoryConnection Inspection as a source container specification.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

query.description: Filter by name
Filter by build status (queued, provisioning, building, failed, build_complete)
The limit of items to return. Default is 20. Max is 5000.
Load the next page of data starting after the item with the given ID.
If true (default), includes total_count in the response. Set to false to skip the count query for better performance on large datasets.

request.description: List all public Blueprints that are available to all users.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

query.description: Filter by name
Filter by build status (queued, provisioning, building, failed, build_complete)
The limit of items to return. Default is 20. Max is 5000.
Load the next page of data starting after the item with the given ID.
If true (default), includes total_count in the response. Set to false to skip the count query for better performance on large datasets.

item.name: List values for a specific blueprint metadata key.

variable.description: (Required) The metadata key to get values for.

request.description: Returns a list of all values that exist for a specific metadata key across all blueprints.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

request.description: Returns a list of all available metadata keys that can be used for filtering blueprints.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Preview Dockerfile definition for a Blueprint.

request.description: Preview building a Blueprint with the specified configuration. You can take the resulting Dockerfile and test out your build using any local docker tooling.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: List values for a specific public blueprint metadata key.

variable.description: (Required) The metadata key to get values for from public blueprints.

request.description: Returns a list of all values that exist for a specific metadata key across all public blueprints only.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: List available public blueprint metadata keys.

request.description: Returns a list of all available metadata keys from public blueprints only that can be used for filtering.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

request.description: Delete a previously created Blueprint. If a blueprint has dependent snapshots, it cannot be deleted. You can find them by querying: GET /v1/devboxes/disk_snapshots?source_blueprint_id={blueprint_id}.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

response.name: Blueprint has dependent snapshots that must be deleted first. Returns list of snapshot IDs.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

request.description: Get all logs from the building of a Blueprint.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

request.description: Get the details of a previously created Blueprint including the build status.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

request.description: Starts build of custom defined container Blueprint. The Blueprint will begin in the 'provisioning' step and transition to the 'building' step once it is selected off the build queue., Upon build complete it will transition to 'building_complete' if the build is successful.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

query.description: Filter by name
Filter by build status (queued, provisioning, building, failed, build_complete)
The limit of items to return. Default is 20. Max is 5000.
Load the next page of data starting after the item with the given ID.
If true (default), includes total_count in the response. Set to false to skip the count query for better performance on large datasets.
